situational_context: Active rebuild phase with ACWR management challenge. Chronic load climbing (25.2, +42% in 12 days) driven by gravel rides (suffer 55-57) interspersed among e-bike commutes (load 6-14). Binary load distribution — rest/commute days vs high-intensity gravel — causes persistent ACWR overshoot (1.49 today, sustained >1.4 for 7 days). Autonomic markers are positive: HRV stable at 38, RHR improved 53 to 49, sleep adequate (7.55h avg). Weight trending down (106.9 to 104.6 kg in 12d, ~1.4 kg/wk — monitor energy availability). Garmin training readiness consistently flags low on gravel days (25/100) but recovers quickly (75-97 on subsequent days). Pattern: ~2 sur site days/week (e-bike commute) + sporadic gravel rides + weekend activity, constrained by young baby. VO2max running: 38.6 (post-gravel artifact, was 40.9).

recommendations.text: ACWR deload: keep Saturday and Sunday sessions at Z1 recovery intensity (HR <130 bpm). No high-intensity efforts until ACWR normalises below 1.3. The Apr 25 gravel dropping from the 7-day window will help naturally.
Moderate gravel ride intensity going forward: target Z2 (HR 120-140 bpm) rather than Z3+ (HR 145+). Limit to max 1 gravel ride per week until chronic load exceeds 50.
recommendations.measurable: Next gravel ride suffer score <=35, avg HR <=140 bpm

recommendations.text: Monitor weight loss rate: current trajectory ~1.4 kg/week exceeds the 1 kg/week rebuild red flag. Ensure adequate calorie intake on training days (target >=2600 kcal, >=150g protein).
recommendations.measurable: Weight loss rate <=1 kg/week over next 7 days; protein >=150g on >=5 of 7 days

markers_to_watch.reason: Currently at 1.49, sustained above 1.3 for ~7 days. Injury risk elevated.
Dropped 40.9 to 38.6 today, likely acute artifact from high-intensity gravel ride. If persistent, suggests fitness regression.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Loss rate should slow to <1 kg/week
markers_to_watch.reason: Current rate ~1.4 kg/week over 12 days. If sustained, raises energy availability concern during rebuild.
